Petition For Order Confirming John Wongs Appointment As Trustee [Probate Code Sections 17200(A), (B)(10), And 15660(D)
Off Calendar Dec-09-2025 Continued To Mar-10-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Asher Anderson appeared for Respondent and moving party John Wong (via Zoom).
Emile Calhoun appeared for Petitioner Sophia Nicole Wong.
Ciaran O'Sullivan appeared for trust beneficiary Albert Wong.

Judge Russell S. Roeca makes a disclosure of his law firm representing Ms. Calhoun in the past.

Discussion was held regarding the appointment of Bennett Hong to fill vacancy of trustee as a professional fiduciary, that current acting trustee John Wang has served as co-trustee with his mother since 1996, that pro bono mediation was unsuccessful, that parties are not to engage in discovery, and that parties are encouraged to continue settlement discussions.

The Court orders Mr. Anderson to file his petition on or before February 6, 2026. Opposition to petition is to be filed by February 9, 2026. And response to opposition is to be filed by February 13, 2026.

The matter are continued to March 10, 2026 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Russell S. Roeca. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Musto. Not reported.

Petition For Orders To 1)Fill A Vacancy In Th Office Of Trustee, With Professional Fiduciary 2)Require That The New Trustee Post Bond 3)Bring The Trust Under Court Supervision 4)Suspend Any Assumed Authority Of Trrustee De Son Tort, John Wong To Serve As Trustee 5)Instruct The New Trustee To Obtain A Forensic Trust Accounting And The Trustee De Son Tort, John Wong, To Fully Cooperate With This Process
Off Calendar Sep-17-2025 Continued To Dec-09-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Asher Anderson and John Minton appeared for Respondent and moving party John Wong (via Zoom).
Emile Calhoun appeared for and with Petitioner Sophia Nicole Wong.
Ciaran O'Sullivan appeared as newly retained counsel for trust beneficiary Albert Wong.

Discussion was held regarding the temporary trustee that has been authorized now for over 5 years, that the trustee position should be filled by a professional fiduciary Bennett Hong, and that parties are open for pro-bono mediation. Ms. Calhoun to file and order withdrawing the request to place the trust under Court Supervision.

The Parties agreed to participate in mediation. The Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed no later than December 2, 2025. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by December 5, 2025. The matter is continued to December 9, 2025 and the Parties are to return on December 9, 2025 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation. Petition for Orders etc. is also continued to December 9, 2025 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Stephen Murphy. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Belen. Not reported.

Petition For Orders To 1)Fill A Vacancy In Th Office Of Trustee, With Professional Fiduciary 2)Require That The New Trustee Post Bond 3)Bring The Trust Under Court Supervision 4)Suspend Any Assumed Authority Of Trrustee De Son Tort, John Wong To Serve As Trustee 5)Instruct The New Trustee To Obtain A Forensic Trust Accounting And The Trustee De Son Tort, John Wong, To Fully Cooperate With This Process
Off Calendar Aug-20-2025 Continued To Sep-17-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Change Of Venue
Off Calendar Sep-10-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Asher Anderson appeared for Respondent and moving party John Wong (via Zoom).
Robert Hernandez appeared for Petitioner and opposing party Sophia Nicole Wong.
Ciaran O'Sullivan appeared as newly retained counsel for trust beneficiary Albert Wong (via Zoom).

The matter was on calendar for Respondent John Wong's Motion for Change of Venue. Mr. Anderson argued that the petitioner sought orders directing John Wong's conduct, including transfer of title to assets, trust records, and cooperation in preparing an accounting. He contended that if John Wong were not treated as a trustee, the requested relief would require a Probate Code section 850 petition and that such transitory causes of action must be heard in the county of John Wong's residence, San Mateo. He further asserted that John Wong should be treated as a de facto trustee, making San Mateo County the proper venue.

Mr. Hernandez responded that the petition sought only to fill a vacancy in the office of trustee following the death of the prior trustee, not to surcharge John Wong or redress breaches of trust. He argued that any request for John Wong to cooperate with the successor trustee was incidental, that John Wong was not duly appointed, and that Probate Code section 17005(b) applied, making San Francisco the proper venue. He noted the request for an accounting was directed to the successor trustee, not John Wong.

Mr. Anderson reiterated that the relief sought included orders that fell under section 850, regardless of how the petition was styled, and maintained that venue should be in San Mateo County.

The Court took the matter under submission.

Following argument, Mr. O'Sullivan requested that the hearing on the underlying petition, set for August 20, 2025, be continued to allow time to respond, given the uncertainty over venue. Mr. Hernandez stated that if the Court adhered to its tentative ruling, the parties would have 30 days to file responses under applicable law, and requested that any continuance include a deadline for responsive pleadings.

The Court continued the hearing on the petition to fill the trustee vacancy to September 17, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)
